title: Add evidence for academic integrity
what_to_do: Write a short reflection (150-250 words) explaining what counts as academic misconduct in your course context, and give one concrete example of how you would respond appropriately.
artifact: Reflection note (text) + citation note (if you used any external source)
how_verified: Instructor can locate the reflection paragraph that explicitly defines misconduct and states a response rule.

title: Strengthen academic integrity reasoning
what_to_do: Create a checklist for your team workflow: how you cite sources, how you avoid copy-paste, and how you review drafts before submission.
artifact: Integrity checklist (text) + one applied example in a project log
how_verified: Evidence shows a concrete workflow rule and an applied instance.

title: Add evidence for privacy awareness
what_to_do: Add a section to your report describing what data is sensitive, what you removed/anonymized, and how consent/deletion would work.
artifact: Privacy section (text) + anonymization examples
how_verified: Evidence explicitly describes sensitive fields and a concrete protection step.

title: Strengthen privacy controls
what_to_do: Propose a minimum-data policy: what fields are stored, what is optional, and how revocation deletes data.
artifact: One-page policy note + deletion checklist
how_verified: Evidence includes concrete stored fields and deletion steps.

title: Document teamwork contributions
what_to_do: Write a team contribution log that details: (1) your specific responsibilities, (2) how tasks were divided, (3) how you communicated with team members.
artifact: Team contribution log (text) + meeting notes or chat excerpts
how_verified: Evidence shows specific tasks assigned and completed by you, with dates or milestones.

title: Improve teamwork evidence depth
what_to_do: Add concrete examples of conflict resolution, decision-making processes, or peer feedback you gave/received.
artifact: Extended contribution log + peer feedback examples
how_verified: Evidence includes at least one conflict/decision scenario and how it was resolved.

title: Add communication skill evidence
what_to_do: Include a sample of written or verbal communication: presentation slides, written report section, or meeting summary you created.
artifact: Communication artifact (slides, text, or recording link)
how_verified: Evidence shows clear structure, audience awareness, and professional tone.

title: Strengthen communication evidence
what_to_do: Add multiple communication samples across different formats (written, visual, verbal) or audiences (technical vs non-technical).
artifact: Multiple communication artifacts with audience context
how_verified: Evidence shows adaptation to different audiences or formats.

title: Document critical thinking process
what_to_do: Write a decision analysis that shows: (1) the problem/question, (2) alternatives considered, (3) evaluation criteria, (4) your reasoning for the final choice.
artifact: Decision analysis document (text)
how_verified: Evidence shows explicit criteria and logical reasoning connecting criteria to decision.

title: Deepen critical analysis
what_to_do: Add counterarguments you considered, limitations of your approach, or how you would decide differently with more information.
artifact: Extended analysis with limitations section
how_verified: Evidence includes explicit acknowledgment of trade-offs or uncertainties.

title: Document problem-solving approach
what_to_do: Describe a specific problem you solved: (1) what the problem was, (2) steps you took to diagnose it, (3) solution implemented, (4) outcome/result.
artifact: Problem-solving case study (text)
how_verified: Evidence shows clear problem definition, systematic approach, and measurable outcome.

title: Strengthen problem-solving evidence
what_to_do: Add multiple problem-solving examples or show how you iterated on an initial solution after testing/feedback.
artifact: Multiple case studies or iteration log
how_verified: Evidence shows either breadth (multiple problems) or depth (iteration/refinement).

title: Add technical writing sample
what_to_do: Include a technical document you wrote: API documentation, system design doc, user guide, or technical report section.
artifact: Technical document (text or markdown)
how_verified: Evidence shows proper structure, technical accuracy, and appropriate detail level.

title: Improve technical documentation
what_to_do: Add documentation for different audiences (developer vs user) or different formats (reference vs tutorial).
artifact: Multiple technical documents with audience specification
how_verified: Evidence shows adaptation to different documentation needs.

title: Document data analysis work
what_to_do: Describe a data analysis you performed: (1) data source, (2) analysis method, (3) key findings, (4) how findings were used.
artifact: Data analysis report (text) + code/queries if applicable
how_verified: Evidence shows clear methodology and actionable conclusions.

title: Strengthen data analysis depth
what_to_do: Add validation steps, sensitivity analysis, or show how you handled data quality issues.
artifact: Extended analysis with validation section
how_verified: Evidence includes explicit data quality checks or uncertainty quantification.

title: Add coding evidence
what_to_do: Include a code sample you wrote with comments explaining: (1) what it does, (2) why you made key design decisions, (3) how to use it.
artifact: Annotated code sample (text or repository link)
how_verified: Evidence shows working code with clear documentation and rationale.

title: Strengthen coding evidence
what_to_do: Add evidence of code quality practices: tests, error handling, refactoring examples, or code review feedback.
artifact: Code with tests, error handling, or review history
how_verified: Evidence shows professional coding practices beyond basic functionality.

title: Document project management
what_to_do: Describe your project management approach: (1) how you planned/scoped, (2) how you tracked progress, (3) how you handled changes/risks.
artifact: Project management summary (text) + planning artifacts
how_verified: Evidence shows structured planning and tracking methodology.

title: Strengthen project management evidence
what_to_do: Add examples of scope changes you managed, risks you mitigated, or stakeholder communication you led.
artifact: Change/risk log or stakeholder communication samples
how_verified: Evidence shows adaptive management beyond initial planning.

title: Add research methodology evidence
what_to_do: Describe your research approach: (1) research question, (2) methods used, (3) sources consulted, (4) how you synthesized findings.
artifact: Research methodology section (text) + bibliography
how_verified: Evidence shows systematic literature review or data collection approach.

title: Strengthen research evidence
what_to_do: Add critical evaluation of sources, comparison of methodologies, or reflection on research limitations.
artifact: Extended research analysis with critical evaluation
how_verified: Evidence shows evaluation of source quality and methodological choices.

title: Add Python programming evidence
what_to_do: Include a Python script or notebook you wrote: (1) what it does, (2) how you ran or tested it, (3) one design choice you made.
artifact: Python code (file or repo link) + short readme or comments
how_verified: Evidence shows runnable Python code and clear purpose.

title: Strengthen Python evidence
what_to_do: Add a second Python example (e.g. data processing, API use, or tests) or document how you debugged or improved existing code.
artifact: Additional code sample or change log with before/after
how_verified: Evidence shows multiple uses of Python or documented refinement.

title: Add R programming evidence
what_to_do: Include an R script or R Markdown you wrote: (1) data source and goal, (2) key steps (e.g. tidy, visualize, model), (3) one finding or output.
artifact: R script or .Rmd (file or repo link) + short description
how_verified: Evidence shows R code and a clear analysis or visualization.

title: Strengthen R evidence
what_to_do: Add a second R project (e.g. Shiny app, package use, or reproducible report) or document how you validated or improved your analysis.
artifact: Additional R artifact or one-page methodology note
how_verified: Evidence shows broader or more reproducible R use.

title: Add SQL and database evidence
what_to_do: Include a sample of SQL you wrote: (1) purpose (e.g. report, extract, check), (2) tables used, (3) one non-trivial part (e.g. JOIN, filter, aggregate).
artifact: SQL file or snippet + one-sentence context
how_verified: Evidence shows executable SQL and clear intent.

title: Strengthen SQL evidence
what_to_do: Add a second example (e.g. multi-table query, subquery, or schema change) or a short note on how you optimized or validated a query.
artifact: Additional SQL or one-page design/optimization note
how_verified: Evidence shows more advanced or validated SQL use.

title: Add data visualization evidence
what_to_do: Include a chart or dashboard you created: (1) data source and question, (2) tool used (e.g. Excel, Tableau, Python, R), (3) one design choice (e.g. chart type, color).
artifact: Screenshot or link to visualization + short caption
how_verified: Evidence shows a clear visual and purpose.

title: Strengthen data visualization evidence
what_to_do: Add a second visualization (e.g. different audience or tool) or a short note on how you improved clarity or accessibility.
artifact: Additional visual or one-page design rationale
how_verified: Evidence shows multiple or improved visualizations.
